title: Instruction on Self-Realization
summary: Janaka asks how to attain knowledge, detachment, and liberation. Ashtavakra answers: you are pure awareness, the witness beyond body and mind.

title: Joy of Self-Realization
summary: Janaka declares his radiant nature upon realizing he is infinite consciousness. He marvels at the joy of knowing the Self.

title: Test of Self-Realization
summary: Ashtavakra tests Janaka's realization by describing how the knower of truth is unattached, solitary, and beyond duality.

title: Glorification of Self-Realization
summary: Ashtavakra glorifies the liberated one who sees all phenomena as the play of consciousness and remains unaffected.

title: Four Ways to Dissolution
summary: Ashtavakra describes four approaches to dissolving the sense of separateness and abiding as the infinite Self.

title: The Higher Knowledge
summary: The Self is beyond space, time, and form. Recognition of one's unbounded nature is the higher knowledge.

title: Nature of Self-Realization
summary: The infinite ocean of consciousness appears to move in the waves of the world, but in truth remains unchanged.

title: Bondage and Liberation
summary: Bondage is clinging to the world as real; liberation is recognizing the world as unreal. Both exist only in the mind.

title: Detachment
summary: Ashtavakra teaches that knowing when actions arise and cease, and remaining indifferent to pairs of opposites, brings freedom.

title: Dispassion
summary: True dispassion arises when one gives up desire for sense objects, recognizing rest in the Self alone as fulfillment.

title: Wisdom
summary: Ashtavakra describes the nature of existence, dissolution, and the wise one who sees through all appearances to the unchanging Self.

title: Abiding in the Self
summary: The sage abides effortlessly in the Self, free from effort, meditation, and practice — simply being what one already is.

title: Happiness
summary: True happiness belongs to the one who has abandoned all, who rests in the Self beyond doing and non-doing.

title: Tranquility
summary: The sage lives in natural tranquility, indifferent to activity and stillness, seeing neither merit nor demerit anywhere.

title: Knowledge of the Self
summary: Ashtavakra contrasts the rare soul who desires liberation with the many attached to the body. The wise one rests free of all.

title: Special Instruction
summary: Ashtavakra gives pointed instruction: abandon clinging to experience, let go of all, and you will at once be free.

title: The True Knower
summary: The true knower is content, purified of desire, and indifferent to praise and blame. Nothing in the world binds such a one.

title: Peace
summary: The longest chapter enumerates the qualities and vision of the liberated being who abides in peace beyond all states and conditions.

title: Repose in the Self
summary: Janaka rests in his true nature, declaring he has dissolved all teachings, perceptions, and even the sense of being a knower.

title: Liberation-in-Life
summary: Janaka celebrates total freedom while living — beyond knowledge and ignorance, beyond liberation and bondage themselves.
